title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Go Client Systems · Go Semantics Case: Decompression Expansion
summary: A backup agent uploads multi-gigabyte artifacts and downloads compressed manifests without unbounded memory or leaked bodies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go decompression expansion increment in a Go 1.26 net/http bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend against decompression bombs, oversized structured values, deep nesting, and unbounded field counts. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Reason about known lengths, HTTP/1.1 chunked delimitation, end of stream, and trailers without exposing framing details as semantics. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Decompression Expansion · Theory Decompression Expansion
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model streams a 12 MiB upload and receives a 40 KiB compressed body that expands past 30 MiB. Define one-shot ownership, replay, compressed and decoded limits, cancellation, trailers, and cleanup. Competency probe: Defend against decompression bombs, oversized structured values, deep nesting, and unbounded field counts. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
